places.name: Promenade des Anglais
places.description: Promenade des Anglais is a promenade along the Mediterranean coast of Nice, France. It extends from Nice Côte d'Azur Airport on the west to the Quai des États-Unis on the east, for a distance of approximately 7 kilometres (4.3 mi). It forms part of Route nationale 98, which runs between Toulon and Menton.
places.whyVisit: Stroll along the azure coast, take in the sea breeze, and experience the quintessential spirit of the French Riviera.

places.name: Colline du Château (Castle Hill)
places.description: A historic hilltop park offering panoramic views over the Baie des Anges, Vieux Nice, and the port, featuring a dramatic artificial waterfall.
places.whyVisit: The absolute best vantage point in Nice for photos, relaxing walks, and viewing the spectacular waterfall.

places.name: Vieux Nice (Old Town)
places.description: A labyrinth of narrow, colorful medieval streets filled with lively plazas, Baroque churches, and artisanal boutiques.
places.whyVisit: To immerse yourself in the historic Italian-influenced architecture, try local street food, and feel the vibrant neighborhood energy.

places.name: Cours Saleya Flower Market
places.description: A bustling open-air market in the heart of the Old Town, selling fresh flowers, local produce, and Niçois specialties like socca.
places.whyVisit: To experience the vibrant colors, delightful scents, and local culinary artisan craft of the French Riviera.

places.name: Musée Matisse
places.description: A 17th-century Genoese villa in the Cimiez district housing an extensive collection of works by French artist Henri Matisse.
places.whyVisit: Trace the artistic evolution of one of modern art's most celebrated masters in the city where he lived and died.

places.name: Musée Marc Chagall
places.description: The Marc Chagall National Museum is a French national museum dedicated to the work of painter Marc Chagall – particularly his works inspired by religion – located in Nice in the Alpes-Maritimes.
places.whyVisit: Admire the incredible stained-glass windows and monumental paintings designed by Chagall himself to fit this exact venue.

places.name: Place Masséna
places.description: The Place Masséna is a historic square in Nice, Alpes-Maritimes, France. It was named for André Masséna. Its layout was designed by Joseph Vernier in 1843-1844.
places.whyVisit: It is the vibrant, beating heart of the city connecting the old town with the modern high street, perfect for people watching.

places.name: Promenade du Paillon (Coulée Verte)
places.description: A sprawling, modern urban park featuring water mirror fountains, playgrounds, and botanical species from all over the world.
places.whyVisit: A refreshing green escape in the middle of the city with fantastic play areas and misting fountains for hot days.

places.name: MAMAC (Musée d'Art Moderne et d'Art Contemporain)
places.description: An avant-garde architectural monument housing masterpieces of European and American contemporary art from the 1950s to the present.
places.whyVisit: Explore works by local stars Yves Klein and Niki de Saint Phalle, and enjoy 360-degree views of Nice from the rooftop walkway.

places.name: Cathédrale Sainte-Réparate
places.description: A breathtaking 17th-century Baroque cathedral located in Vieux Nice, dedicated to the patron saint of the city.
places.whyVisit: Marvel at the ornate interior plasterwork, gold gilding, ten beautiful chapels, and the prominent tiled dome visible from the hills.

places.name: Palais Lascaris
places.description: A magnificent 17th-century aristocratic mansion in Vieux Nice displaying monumental frescoes, tapestries, and a rare collection of historical musical instruments.
places.whyVisit: Step back in time to the height of Genoese Baroque opulence in a beautifully restored noble palace.

places.name: Villa Masséna Musée
places.description: An elegant Belle Époque mansion on the Promenade des Anglais showing the glamorous history of Riviera tourism and Napoleon's local ties.
places.whyVisit: See Josephine’s golden tiara and explore the history of Nice from its 19th-century boom to its transition into a winter resort capital.

places.name: Port Lympia (Nice Port)
places.description: The historic harbor of Nice, featuring colorful Venetian-style facades, grand yachts, and traditional wooden pointu fishing boats.
places.whyVisit: Walk the scenic docks, watch local fisherman unload their catch, and admire the upscale yachts contrasting with historic architecture.

places.name: Parc Phoenix
places.description: A 7-hectare botanical and animal park with one of the largest greenhouses in Europe, showcasing various tropical ecosystems.
places.whyVisit: A paradise for children featuring free-roaming exotic birds, small mammals, rare orchids, and an adventure play area.

places.name: Basilique Notre-Dame de l'Assomption
places.description: An imposing 19th-century neo-Gothic basilica on Avenue Jean Médecin, featuring elegant twin towers and striking stained glass windows.
places.whyVisit: The largest church in Nice, displaying a stark, beautiful contrast to the city's ubiquitous Italian Baroque style.

places.name: Chez René Socca
places.description: A legendary historic eatery in Old Nice famous for its traditional Niçois street food including socca (chickpea pancake) and pissaladière.
places.whyVisit: The most authentic, casual spot to try Niçois specialty cuisine fresh from the wood-fired ovens.

places.name: Opéra de Nice
places.description: A prestigious, ornate 19th-century Italianate opera house located near the seafront, offering classical performances.
places.whyVisit: To appreciate world-class opera, ballet, or symphonic performances within a majestic historic auditorium with unparalleled acoustics.

places.name: La Crypte Archéologique de Nice
places.description: An immense underground chamber discovered during tram excavations, preserving the 14th-century fortifications of the city.
places.whyVisit: Walk over elevated metal gantries to view remarkably preserved historical ramparts underneath the modern city streets.

places.name: Le Negresco
places.description: The legendary palatial hotel overlooking the sea, a majestic showcase of Belle Époque architecture housing museum-quality art.
places.whyVisit: Even if you are not staying, viewing this national historic monument's pink dome and ornate facades from the outside is a visual treat.

places.name: Cave de la Tour
places.description: One of the oldest bistros and wine cellars in Nice, offering local wines and a vintage neighborhood atmosphere.
places.whyVisit: Taste rare local wines from Bellet under wood beams in a cozy, authentic Niçois pub dating back to 1947.

hotels.name: Hyatt Regency Nice Palais de la Méditerranée
Hotel La Pérouse Nice Baie des Anges
